allowed_study_structuring: parallel notes/comparison beside the text only
not_allowed_transformation: silent rewriting, normalization, or chunking as if it were the original witness text
why: The witness layer must stay faithful to how the scholar-book rendered the proof.

allowed_study_structuring: study-friendly chunking and memorization-oriented presentation derived from the authoritative text
not_allowed_transformation: meaning-changing rewrite or loss of source traceability
why: This is the right layer for study-friendly structuring because it is also the direct memorization layer.

not_allowed_transformation: pretending analytic summaries are the original proof wording
why: Help is allowed as a separate layer, not as a replacement for source text.

allowed_study_structuring: secondary aid only, clearly marked as generated
not_allowed_transformation: becoming the authoritative proof or silently standing in for the preserved source
why: Generated aid may help study, but it must never overwrite source integrity.
